window_summary: This window tracks the recent Unitech/pm2 release stream from version 4.4.1 through v7.0.1, covering feature additions, bug fixes, dependency updates, and major architectural changes including the introduction of system monitoring, Bun support, and a core refactor in v7.0.0.

chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Unitech/pm2
Release: 4.4.1
Tag: 4.4.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-08-17T08:51:45Z; created_at: 2020-08-17T08:48:38Z
Release notes:
- feature drop: autodump fixes #4763
- fix: fix starting pm2 script from inside an app #4761 (need use PM2_PROGRAMMATIC)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Unitech/pm2 release 4.4.1, the release notes include a feature drop for autodump (fixes #4763) and a fix for starting pm2 script from inside an app (#4761, requiring PM2_PROGRAMMATIC).

chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Unitech/pm2
Release: 4.5.0
Tag: 4.5.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-09-29T14:25:11Z; created_at: 2020-09-29T13:03:59Z
Release notes:
## 4.5.0
- fix: creating log folder in sync mode. #4846 - PR #4844 by @QS20199
- fix: PM2 unable to run ESM packages: ERR_UNSUPPORTED_ESM_URL_SCHEME #4839 - PR #4841 by @ox-harris
- fix: Use opts.namespace if it was passed in. Previously, it was ignored. #4778 - PR #4791 by @sbleon
- fix: Prevent reloadLogs command from hanging. #4781 - PR #4782 by @mbrooks and Franck Danard
- fix: backward compatibility fix for tarball modules - PR #4767 by @ykolbin
- fix: Operation not permitted on call setgid #2957 - PR #4681 by @guard43ru
- chore: upgrade dependencies
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Unitech/pm2 release 4.5.0, the release notes include fixes for creating log folder in sync mode (#4846), running ESM packages (#4839), using opts.namespace (#4778), preventing reloadLogs hang (#4781), backward compatibility for tarball modules (#4767), and Operation not permitted on setgid (#2957), along with dependency upgrades.

chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Unitech/pm2
Release: 4.5.1
Tag: 4.5.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-12-16T14:36:52Z; created_at: 2020-12-16T14:34:12Z
Release notes:
- fix: cron in cluster mode was not restarting process after one pass - fixes #4834 #4733 #4307 #4834
- fix: restore --sort <param> option on `pm2 ls` - fix #4536
- fix: fix tests with npm7
- fix: restore warning message about process list changed and not dumped
- chore: alias `--cron` with `--cron-restart`
- chore: test PM2 againt Node.js 15.x
- chore: upgrade systeminformation and debug module to latest - PR #4892 by @AdamMajer
- chore: drop Node 9.x from travis testing
- chore: remove ps-list module
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Unitech/pm2 release 4.5.1, the release notes include fixes for cron in cluster mode not restarting process after one pass (#4834), restoring --sort option on pm2 ls (#4536), fixing tests with npm7, restoring warning about process list not dumped, aliasing --cron with --cron-restart, testing against Node.js 15.x, upgrading systeminformation and debug modules, dropping Node 9.x from travis testing, and removing ps-list module.

Repository: Unitech/pm2
Release: 5.0.0
Tag: 5.0.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2021-06-07T13:43:59Z; created_at: 2021-06-07T13:08:45Z
Release notes:
### System Monitoring
A new local system monitoring feature has been added, allowing to monitor numerous vital server metrics.
Most important metrics will be displayed when doing a pm2 ls:
```bash
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ id  в”Ӯ name            в”Ӯ namespace   в”Ӯ version в”Ӯ mode    в”Ӯ pid      в”Ӯ uptime в”Ӯ вҶә    в”Ӯ status    в”Ӯ cpu      в”Ӯ mem      в”Ӯ user     в”Ӯ watching в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ 4   в”Ӯ app             в”Ӯ default     в”Ӯ 1.0.0   в”Ӯ fork    в”Ӯ 164618   в”Ӯ 2s     в”Ӯ 1670 в”Ӯ online    в”Ӯ 0%       в”Ӯ 41.8mb   в”Ӯ unitech  в”Ӯ disabled в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
host metrics | cpu: 1.6% 42.9Вә | mem free: 52.0% | wlp0s20f3: вҮ“ 0mb/s вҮ‘ 0mb/s | disk: вҮ“ 0.199mb/s вҮ‘ 0mb/s /dev/nvme0n1p3 88.25% |
```
All server metrics will be available to pm2.io:
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ PM2 CPU Usage               в”Ӯ 0.0 %                            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ PM2 Memory Usage            в”Ӯ 67.4 mb                          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ PM2 Agent CPU Usage         в”Ӯ 0 %                              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ PM2 Agent Memory Usage      в”Ӯ 0 mb                             в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ CPU Usage                   в”Ӯ 1.5 %                            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ CPUs Usage                  в”Ӯ 0|0|0|0|2|0|0|1|0|0|0|1|0|1|2|8  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ CPU Temperature             в”Ӯ 42.9 В°C                          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ RAM Total                   в”Ӯ 15.34 gb                         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ RAM Free                    в”Ӯ 1.18 gb                          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ RAM Active                  в”Ӯ 7.35 gb                          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ RAM Available               в”Ӯ 7.99 gb                          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ RAM Usage                   в”Ӯ 47.9 %                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ FD Opened                   в”Ӯ 15072                            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Disk Writes                 в”Ӯ 0 mb/s                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Disk Reads                  в”Ӯ 0.24 mb/s                        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Disk Usage                  в”Ӯ 88.25 %                          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Disk Size                   в”Ӯ 465.60 gb                        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Total TX                    в”Ӯ 0.005 mb/s                       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Total RX                    в”Ӯ 0.004 mb/s                       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ fs:use:/dev/nvme0n1p3       в”Ӯ 88.25 %                          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ fs:size:/dev/nvme0n1p3      в”Ӯ 465.60 gb                        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ net:tx_5:wlp0s20f3          в”Ӯ 0.005 mb/s                       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ net:rx_5:wlp0s20f3          в”Ӯ 0.004 mb/s                       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ net:rx_errors_60:wlp0s20f3  в”Ӯ 0 /min                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ net:tx_errors_60:wlp0s20f3  в”Ӯ 0 /min                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ net:rx_dropped_60:wlp0s20f3 в”Ӯ 0 /min                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ net:tx_dropped_60:wlp0s20f3 в”Ӯ 0 /min                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ graphics:mem:total          в”Ӯ 3878 mb                          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ graphics:mem:used           в”Ӯ 1260 mb                          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ graphics:temp               в”Ӯ 46 В°C                            в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```
#### Disabling system monitoring
```
# Disable system monitoring
pm2 set pm2:sysmonit false
# Enable system monitoring
pm2 set pm2:sysmonit true
```
### PM2.io bandwidth reduction
Data quantity sent from PM2 to PM2.io has been reduced by 80%, thanks for a json patch differential system. Much more data can now be exposed (metrics, actions) to PM2.io
### Other fixes
- feat: added args and full script path to monitoring data
- fix: regular local ip check
- fix: pm2 agent watchdog has been consolidated and fixes freezing issues
- fix: modules bumped
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Unitech/pm2 release 5.0.0, the release notes introduce a new local system monitoring feature displaying vital server metrics in pm2 ls and pm2.io, a bandwidth reduction for PM2.io data via json patch differential, and other fixes including added args and full script path to monitoring data, regular local ip check, consolidated pm2 agent watchdog fixing freezing issues, and bumped modules.

chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Unitech/pm2
Release: 5.2.0
Tag: 5.2.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2022-02-17T13:05:04Z; created_at: 2022-02-17T09:25:38Z
Release notes:
- replace node-cron by croner (#5183 #5035)
- upgrade mocha deps
- fix pm2 report when daemon not running
- remove semver check for legacy node.js versions
- update node version in setup.deb.sh by using lts (#5201) + openrc
- replace legacy util._extend by Object.assign (#5239)
- add missing start options types (#5242)
- recursive detection of package.json (#5267)
- make tarball module uninstall cross-platform (#5269)
- Fix unnecessary "ENOENT" console.error when serving a spa (#5272)
- fix: used env variable instead of hardcode datetime format (#5277)
- copyright update (#5278)
- fix: remove constants import from VersionCheck (not needed) (#5279)
- Reduce async import (#5280)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Unitech/pm2 release 5.2.0, the release notes include replacing node-cron by croner, upgrading mocha deps, fixing pm2 report when daemon not running, removing semver check for legacy Node.js versions, updating node version in setup.deb.sh, replacing legacy util._extend by Object.assign, adding missing start options types, recursive detection of package.json, making tarball module uninstall cross-platform, fixing unnecessary ENOENT console.error when serving a spa, using env variable instead of hardcoded datetime format, copyright update, removing constants import from VersionCheck, and reducing async import.

Repository: Unitech/pm2
Release: v6.0.7

chunks.title: v7.0.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Unitech/pm2
Release: v7.0.0
Tag: v7.0.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-05-02T07:11:59Z; created_at: 2026-05-02T07:07:23Z
Release notes:
## 7.0.0
### Breaking Changes
- Require Node.js >= 18.0.0 (dropped Node.js 16 support)
### Core Refactor
- Internalize pm2-axon, pm2-axon-rpc, pm2-io-bpm, pm2-io-agent, fclone as local modules (reduced supply chain surface)
- Internalize pm2-multimeter and charm into lib/tools/multimeter (zero external deps)
- Add Bun runtime support (ProcessContainerBun.js, ProcessContainerForkBun.js)
- Replace `needle` with native `fetch` (CliAuth, TAR publish)
- Replace `enquirer` with lightweight built-in prompt (boilerplate selector)
- Replace `promptly` with built-in lib/tools/prompt
- Replace `mkdirp` with native `fs.mkdirSync({ recursive: true })`
- Replace `source-map-support` with native `process.setSourceMapsEnabled()`
- Replace `sprintf-js` with template literals (Dashboard)
- Replace `url.parse()` with native `URL` constructor (Serve, Utility, CliAuth)
- Remove `fclone` npm dep, use internalized module
- Drop auto source map file detection in Common.prepareAppConf
### Security
- CVE-2025-5891 Fix ReDoS in Config.js string-to-array split regex #6075
- CVE-2026-27699 Update proxy-agent to 6.5.0, basic-ftp to 5.3.1 #6088
- Fix command injection in WebAuth.js open() вҖ” replace exec() with execFile() #6089
- Fix command injection in PM2IO.js open() вҖ” replace exec() with execFile(), validate SUDO_USER
- Fix command injection in lib/tools/open.js вҖ” replace exec() with execFile(), validate SUDO_USER
- Fix prototype pollution in Configuration.set/unset via __proto__ key traversal #6089
- Fix HttpInterface env stripping never executing (WEB_STRIP_ENV_VARS) #6089
### Bug Fixes
- Rewrite TreeKill: single ps snapshot + in-memory tree build, eliminates race conditions. SIGKILL escalation now targets surviving child processes directly instead of re-walking a dead tree #6084
- Fix [object Object] env vars leaked to fork mode subprocesses #6073
- Fix Windows home path: use os.homedir() instead of HOMEPATH/HOMEDRIVE env vars #6106
- Fix Windows TreeKill callback consistency
- Fix missing BPM monitoring injection in Bun cluster mode (ProcessContainerBun.js)
- Fix ReferenceError crash in Bun cluster console overrides when disable_logs is true
- Fix CliAuth wrong credentials error displaying "undefined" instead of error message
### Features
- Add `--ftp` option to `pm2 serve` for directory listing (python http.server style)
### Dependencies
- Add OpenTelemetry tracing as direct dependencies (@opentelemetry/api, sdk-node, auto-instrumentations-node)
- Upgrade OpenTelemetry packages to latest
- Update pidusage from 3.0.2 to 4.0.1
- Upgrade ws to ^8.18.0, eventemitter2 to ^6.4.9
- Remove needle, enquirer, promptly, mkdirp, source-map-support, sprintf-js, fclone from npm dependencies
### Testing
- Add Docker parallel test runner with Node.js and Bun support
- Add Windows test suite (test/windows.sh)
- Add OpenTelemetry tracing tests
- Add TreeKill unit tests
- Add test scripts for internalized modules (bpm, axon, axon-rpc, io-agent)
- Fix test compatibility for Node.js 22+ and Bun
- CI matrix: Node.js 18, 20 + latest
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Unitech/pm2 release v7.0.0, the release notes include breaking changes requiring Node.js >= 18.0.0, a core refactor internalizing several modules, adding Bun runtime support, replacing various dependencies with native alternatives, security fixes for ReDoS, command injection, and prototype pollution, bug fixes including a TreeKill rewrite, features like --ftp option for pm2 serve, dependency updates including OpenTelemetry tracing, and testing improvements.

chunks.title: v7.0.1
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Unitech/pm2
Release: v7.0.1
Tag: v7.0.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-05-02T13:21:16Z; created_at: 2026-05-02T13:19:49Z
Release notes:
## 7.0.1
### Bug Fixes
- Fix Python (and other non-Node) interpreter regression on Ubuntu: bun runtime detection used a naive `includes('bun')` substring check that matched any path containing the letters "bun" вҖ” most notably `/home/ubuntu/...`. Affected paths were routed through `ProcessContainerForkBun.js` and crashed with `SyntaxError: unterminated string literal` when Python tried to parse the JS container. Anchored the match to the end of the interpreter path (`=== 'bun'` or `/bun$/`) in both `lib/God/ForkMode.js` and `lib/Common.js` #5990
- Display `max_memory_restart` in `pm2 describe` output when set #5925
- Add missing `port` option to `StartOptions` TypeScript declaration #6045
- Fix incorrect file permissions on `openrc.tpl` template (0755 вҶ’ 0644) #5957
- Fix Windows cmd.exe regression: revert `bin/pm2*` launchers to `#!/usr/bin/env node` shebang (was polyglot `#!/bin/sh`). Polyglot worked on Linux/macOS but broke npm's `pm2.cmd` shim on Windows вҖ” `cmd.exe` can't interpret `/bin/sh` shebang and failed with `'"/bin/sh"' is not recognized as an internal or external command`. PowerShell's auto-generated `pm2.ps1` shim happened to call `node` directly so it kept working, masking the regression. Bun-only Linux/macOS users (no Node installed) need to symlink `node` to `bun` (`sudo ln -s $(which bun) /usr/local/bin/node`) вҖ” same workaround used in the project's bun test Dockerfile. Documented in README #6108
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Unitech/pm2 release v7.0.1, the release notes include bug fixes for a Python interpreter regression on Ubuntu caused by bun runtime detection, displaying max_memory_restart in pm2 describe, adding missing port option to TypeScript declaration, fixing incorrect file permissions on openrc.tpl template, and fixing a Windows cmd.exe regression by reverting bin/pm2 launchers to #!/usr/bin/env node shebang.

answer_support.answer_text: introduces a new local system monitoring feature displaying vital server metrics in pm2 ls

questions.query_text: Across the Unitech/pm2 releases, how does the change in v6.0.5 regarding Bun support differ from the change in v7.0.0 regarding Bun support?

options.text: v6.0.5 adds Bun support, while v7.0.0 adds Bun runtime support and internalizes related modules.
v6.0.5 removes Bun support, while v7.0.0 reintroduces it.
Both releases only update Bun-related dependencies without functional changes.
v6.0.5 adds Bun support for cluster mode, while v7.0.0 adds Bun support for fork mode.

questions.expected_answer: v6.0.5 adds Bun support, while v7.0.0 adds Bun runtime support and internalizes related modules.

questions.query_text: In the Unitech/pm2 release window, order the following release-content states from earliest to latest: (1) a release that bumps @pm2/js-api for an axios CVE, (2) a release that adds Bun support and disables git parsing by default, (3) a release that replaces chalk with ansis, (4) a release that drops npm-shrinkwrap in favor of fixed dependencies versions.

answer_support.answer_text: Bun support - Disable git parsing by default
Drop npm-shrinkwrap in favor of fixed dependencies versions
